Base directory for this skill: C:\Users\tjn\.claude\plugins\marketplaces\taches-cc-resources\skills\create-plans

<essential_principles>

<principle name="solo_developer_plus_claude">
You are planning for ONE person (the user) and ONE implementer (Claude).
No teams. No stakeholders. No ceremonies. No coordination overhead.
The user is the visionary/product owner. Claude is the builder.
</principle>

<principle name="plans_are_prompts">
PLAN.md is not a document that gets transformed into a prompt.
PLAN.md IS the prompt. It contains:
- Objective (what and why)
- Context (@file references)
- Tasks (type, files, action, verify, done, checkpoints)
- Verification (overall checks)
- Success criteria (measurable)
- Output (SUMMARY.md specification)

When planning a phase, you are writing the prompt that will execute it.
</principle>

<principle name="scope_control">
Plans must complete within ~50% of context usage to maintain consistent quality.

**The quality degradation curve:**
- 0-30% context: Peak quality (comprehensive, thorough, no anxiety)
- 30-50% context: Good quality (engaged, manageable pressure)
- 50-70% context: Degrading quality (efficiency mode, compression)
- 70%+ context: Poor quality (self-lobotomization, rushed work)

**Critical insight:** Claude doesn't degrade at 80% - it degrades at ~40-50% when it sees context mounting and enters "completion mode." By 80%, quality has already crashed.

**Solution:** Aggressive atomicity - split phases into many small, focused plans.

Examples:
- `01-01-PLAN.md` - Phase 1, Plan 1 (2-3 tasks: database schema only)
- `01-02-PLAN.md` - Phase 1, Plan 2 (2-3 tasks: database client setup)
- `01-03-PLAN.md` - Phase 1, Plan 3 (2-3 tasks: API routes)
- `01-04-PLAN.md` - Phase 1, Plan 4 (2-3 tasks: UI components)

Each plan is independently executable, verifiable, and scoped to **2-3 tasks maximum**.

**Atomic task principle:** Better to have 10 small, high-quality plans than 3 large, degraded plans. Each commit should be surgical, focused, and maintainable.

**Autonomous execution:** Plans without checkpoints execute via subagent with fresh context - impossible to degrade.

See: references/scope-estimation.md
</principle>

<principle name="human_checkpoints">
**Claude automates everything that has a CLI or API.** Checkpoints are for verification and decisions, not manual work.

**Checkpoint types:**
- `checkpoint:human-verify` - Human confirms Claude's automated work (visual checks, UI verification)
- `checkpoint:decision` - Human makes implementation choice (auth provider, architecture)

**Rarely needed:** `checkpoint:human-action` - Only for actions with no CLI/API (email verification links, account approvals requiring web login with 2FA)

**Critical rule:** If Claude CAN do it via CLI/API/tool, Claude MUST do it. Never ask human to:
- Deploy to Vercel/Railway/Fly (use CLI)
- Create Stripe webhooks (use CLI/API)
- Run builds/tests (use Bash)
- Write .env files (use Write tool)
- Create database resources (use provider CLI)

**Protocol:** Claude automates work → reaches checkpoint:human-verify → presents what was done → waits for confirmation → resumes

See: references/checkpoints.md, references/cli-automation.md
</principle>

<principle name="deviation_rules">
Plans are guides, not straitjackets. Real development always involves discoveries.

**During execution, deviations are handled automatically via 5 embedded rules:**

1. **Auto-fix bugs** - Broken behavior → fix immediately, document in Summary
2. **Auto-add missing critical** - Security/correctness gaps → add immediately, document
3. **Auto-fix blockers** - Can't proceed → fix immediately, document
4. **Ask about architectural** - Major structural changes → stop and ask user
5. **Log enhancements** - Nice-to-haves → auto-log to ISSUES.md, continue

**No user intervention needed for Rules 1-3, 5.** Only Rule 4 (architectural) requires user decision.

**All deviations documented in Summary** with: what was found, what rule applied, what was done, commit hash.

**Result:** Flow never breaks. Bugs get fixed. Scope stays controlled. Complete transparency.

See: workflows/execute-phase.md (deviation_rules section)
</principle>

<principle name="ship_fast_iterate_fast">
No enterprise process. No approval gates. No multi-week timelines.
Plan → Execute → Ship → Learn → Repeat.

**Milestone-driven:** Ship v1.0 → mark milestone → plan v1.1 → ship → repeat.
Milestones mark shipped versions and enable continuous iteration.
</principle>

<principle name="milestone_boundaries">
Milestones mark shipped versions (v1.0, v1.1, v2.0).

**Purpose:**
- Historical record in MILESTONES.md (what shipped when)
- Greenfield → Brownfield transition marker
- Git tags for releases
- Clear completion rituals

**Default approach:** Extend existing roadmap with new phases.
- v1.0 ships (phases 1-4) → add phases 5-6 for v1.1
- Continuous phase numbering (01-99)
- Milestone groupings keep roadmap organized

**Archive ONLY for:** Separate codebases or complete rewrites (rare).

See: references/milestone-management.md
</principle>

<principle name="anti_enterprise_patterns">
NEVER include in plans:
- Team structures, roles, RACI matrices
- Stakeholder management, alignment meetings
- Sprint ceremonies, standups, retros
- Multi-week estimates, resource allocation
- Change management, governance processes
- Documentation for documentation's sake

If it sounds like corporate PM theater, delete it.
</principle>

<principle name="context_awareness">
Monitor token usage via system warnings.

**At 25% remaining**: Mention context getting full
**At 15% remaining**: Pause, offer handoff
**At 10% remaining**: Auto-create handoff, stop

Never start large operations below 15% without user confirmation.
</principle>

<principle name="user_gates">
Never charge ahead at critical decision points. Use gates:
- **AskUserQuestion**: Structured choices (2-4 options)
- **Inline questions**: Simple confirmations
- **Decision gate loop**: "Ready, or ask more questions?"

Mandatory gates:
- Before writing PLAN.md (confirm breakdown)
- After low-confidence research
- On verification failures
- After phase completion with issues
- Before starting next phase with previous issues

See: references/user-gates.md
</principle>

<principle name="git_versioning">
All planning artifacts are version controlled. Commit outcomes, not process.

- Check for repo on invocation, offer to initialize
- Commit only at: initialization, phase completion, handoff
- Intermediate artifacts (PLAN.md, RESEARCH.md, FINDINGS.md) NOT committed separately
- Git log becomes project history

See: references/git-integration.md
</principle>

</essential_principles>


**Examples:**
- "Should we use Redux or Zustand?" → `/consider:first-principles`
- "Which features to prioritize?" → `/consider:eisenhower-matrix`
- "Is this the right architecture?" → `/consider:second-order`

→ Analyze decision type and invoke appropriate framework

### 3. Debugging Tasks → `debug-like-expert` skill
**Keywords:** "debug", "intermittent", "mysterious", "sometimes fails", "race condition"
**Example:** "Debug the intermittent calendar sync issue"

→ Invoke: `Skill(debug-like-expert)` with task description

### 4. Moderate Complexity → `/create-prompt` + `/run`
**Criteria:**
- 30min - 2hr estimated work
- 3-10 files to modify
- Clear scope but needs fresh context
**Example:** "Refactor the widget loading system"

→ Invoke: `/create-prompt {{task_description}}` then offer to `/run`

### 5. Multi-Stage Features → `/create-meta-prompt`
**Keywords:** "research then implement", "explore options then build", multi-stage
**Example:** "Research authentication options then implement the best one"

→ Invoke: `/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}`

### 6. Simple/Direct Tasks → Execute directly
**Criteria:**
- 1-2 files
- <30min work
- Clear, straightforward
**Example:** "Fix the typo in the README"

→ Execute directly in current context

## Implementation

**Step 1: Parse Arguments**
```javascript
const taskDescription = "{{task_description}}".trim();

if (!taskDescription) {
  // No task provided - check todos
  return "/check-todos";
}
```

**Step 2: Analyze Task**

Examine task description for:
- **Keywords** indicating task type
- **Complexity signals** (file count, time estimate, scope)
- **Execution pattern** (single-stage, multi-stage, research-heavy)

**Step 3: Route to Optimal Workflow**

Based on analysis:

```javascript
// Planning tasks
if (matches("plan|design|architecture|multi-phase|roadmap")) {
  invoke("/create-plan {{task_description}}");
}

// Decision-making tasks
else if (matches("should we|which|evaluate|compare|decide")) {
  determineFramework(); // first-principles, eisenhower-matrix, etc.
  invoke("/consider:[framework] {{task_description}}");
}

// Debugging tasks
else if (matches("debug|intermittent|mysterious|race condition")) {
  invoke("Skill(debug-like-expert)");
}

// Moderate complexity - delegate to fresh context
else if (estimatedComplexity === "moderate") {
  invoke("/create-prompt {{task_description}}");
  // Offer to run after creation
}

// Multi-stage features
else if (matches("research then|explore then|multi-stage")) {
  invoke("/create-meta-prompt {{task_description}}");
}

// Simple/direct - execute in current context
else {
  executeDirectly();
}
```

**Step 4: Follow Through**

After routing:
- If created prompt/plan: offer to execute with `/run` or `/run-plan`
- If direct execution: follow standard workflow (plan → approve → implement)
- If framework: present analysis and next steps

## Examples

```bash
# Automatically routes to /create-plan
/start-task Plan the user authentication system

# Automatically routes to /consider:first-principles
/start-task Should we use WebSockets or Server-Sent Events?

# Automatically routes to debug-like-expert
/start-task Debug the intermittent widget loading failure

# Automatically routes to /create-prompt
/start-task Refactor the calendar view components

# Automatically routes to /create-meta-prompt
/start-task Research GraphQL options then implement the best approach

# Direct execution
/start-task Fix typo in README.md

# Check todos (no argument)
/start-task
```

## Notes

- **Transparent routing:** User just describes task naturally
- **Zero configuration:** Analysis and routing happen automatically
- **Optimal execution:** Each task routed to best-fit workflow
- **CLAUDE.md compliant:** Follows Intelligent Prompt Routing specification
- **Composable:** Routes to other slash commands and skills as needed


ARGUMENTS: Continue
I'll continue with Forms Advanced Features (P0 gap) by creating a focused, executable task file and implementing it phase by phase.

# Master Implementation Status - Neuman Brain Dashboard
**Created:** 2025-11-26
**Last Updated:** 2025-11-29 (Merged from 4 versions)
**Purpose:** Comprehensive audit of ALL features across user notes, research docs, and competitive analysis
**Status:** Draft - Awaiting User Approval
**Source Documents:**
- `tasks/Neuman Brain User Notes 11252025.txt` (User testing feedback)
- `tasks/research-document.md` (26 feature categories researched)
- `tasks/research-document-expanded-initial-dig.md` (50+ competitor analysis)

---

## Table of Contents
1. [Implementation Status Legend](#implementation-status-legend)
2. [Completed Features (100% Parity)](#completed-features-100-parity)
3. [Partial Implementation (<100% Parity)](#partial-implementation-100-parity)
4. [Missing All-in-One Platform Features](#missing-all-in-one-platform-features)
5. [Productivity Widget Ideas](#productivity-widget-ideas)
6. [Not Started](#not-started)
7. [Research Needed](#research-needed)
8. [Foundation & Infrastructure](#foundation--infrastructure)
9. [Competitor Feature Matrix](#competitor-feature-matrix)
10. [Provider Directory & Ecosystem Map (2025)](#provider-directory--ecosystem-map-2025)

---

## Implementation Status Legend

- **COMPLETED (100%)** - Feature fully implemented with all desired functionality
- **PARTIAL (<100%)** - Feature exists but missing key capabilities or polish
- **NOT STARTED** - Feature identified but no implementation yet
- **RESEARCH NEEDED** - Requires investigation before implementation planning

---

## COMPLETED FEATURES (100% Parity)

### Dashboard & Widgets
✅ **Task Summary Widget** - Dynamically updates with Quick Add (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Upcoming Events Widget** - Height fixed, displays upcoming events (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Quick Add Task Button** - Color contrast fixed (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Widget Layout System** - Flexible dashboard widget grid

### Schedule & Calendar
✅ **ICS Calendar Import** - Date format fixed, now working (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Calendar "Today" Highlighting** - Current day clearly marked
✅ **Double-Click Day Behavior** - Opens DayDetailModal with events/tasks (Fixed 2025-11-25)
  - Shows all events for selected day
  - Shows all tasks due that day with navigation
  - "Add New Event" button
  - Goes straight to add if empty
✅ **Kanban Tasks on Calendar** - Tasks with due dates appear on calendar (Fixed 2025-11-25)
  - Blue styling to distinguish from events
  - Shows title, priority, status
  - Clickable in DayDetailModal
✅ **Monthly Calendar Unification** - Summary tab calendar matches Calendar tab style (Fixed 2025-11-26)
  - Added min-h-[80px] cells
  - Clickable days open detail modal
  - Shows time entries for selected day
✅ **Sidebar Navigation** - Expandable children for Schedule sections
✅ **Event Start Date Picker** - Events can be created for any date (Fixed 2025-11-26)
  - Editable start date field
  - Defaults to clicked calendar day
  - End date validation

### Notes
✅ **Folders Sidebar** - Button overlap fixed (Fixed 2025-11-25, Enhanced 2025-11-26)
  - Collapse button at right edge (no overlap)
  - Expand button when collapsed
  - Resize functionality with drag handle
  - Width persists 200-500px range
✅ **Notes CRUD** - Create, read, update, delete notes
✅ **Folder Organization** - Organize notes in folders
✅ **Daily Notes Pattern** - Obsidian/Roam-style daily journaling (Implemented 2025-11-26)
  - Keyboard shortcut (Ctrl/Cmd + D) to open today's note
  - Auto-create with customizable templates
  - Calendar view showing daily notes indicators
  - Settings UI: date format, template editor, folder selection
  - Auto-links to yesterday/tomorrow notes
  - Template variables: {date}, {yesterday}, {tomorrow}, {weekday}

### Settings
✅ **Display Name Autofill Prevention** - autoComplete="off" added (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Auto-Save Folder Display** - Shows configured folder correctly (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Backup Filename Format** - Shows "Neuman" with ".brain" outside (Fixed 2025-11-25)
✅ **Privacy Footer Cleanup** - Removed duplicate footer (Fixed 2025-11-25)

### Foundation & Data Integrity (2025-11-25)
✅ **Data Validation Layer** - src/services/validation.ts
  - Centralized Zod schemas
  - Date key format validation (YYYY-M-D)
  - Time format validation (HH:mm)
  - Legacy date normalization
✅ **Error Boundaries** - src/components/StoreErrorBoundary.tsx
  - Recovery UI with Try Again/Clear Data/Restore
  - Wrapped critical routes
✅ **Import Safety** - src/services/brainBackup.ts
  - Pre-import validation
  - Rollback snapshot
  - Automatic date normalization
  - Invalid data filtered

### UI Polish (2025-11-26)
✅ **Modal Overflow Fixes**
  - CardDetailPanel: Removed nested scroll
  - QuickAddModal: Added max-h-[85vh]
  - Proper content containment
✅ **MonthlyTimeReport Day Detail Modal**
  - Click any day to see entries
  - Shows project colors, descriptions, durations
  - "Add Time Entry" button

### AI & Terminal
✅ **AI Terminal Shell** - Basic shell emulation with error messaging
✅ **Error Messaging** - Clear explanations for unsupported commands

### Tasks & Project Management
✅ **Task CRUD** - Create, read, update, delete tasks
✅ **Task Due Dates** - Tasks can have due dates (start + due dates supported)
✅ **Task Priorities** - Priority levels (low/medium/high) with color coding
✅ **Task Status** - Status tracking with customizable columns

---

## PARTIAL IMPLEMENTATION (<100% Parity)

### Tasks & Project Management
🔶 **Kanban Board** (Status: PARTIAL - Reclassified 2025-11-28)
- **Audit Date:** 2025-11-28
- **Competitors Analyzed:** Trello, Asana Board View, Monday.com Kanban View
- **Current Features:**
  - ✅ Drag-and-drop between columns (@dnd-kit with pointer + touch sensors)
  - ✅ Customizable columns (ColumnManager, 3-7 column view options)
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) with search support - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Priority levels (low/medium/high) with color coding
  - ✅ Tags with autocomplete and filtering
  - ✅ Start dates + due dates
  - ✅ Checklists with real-time persistence
  - ✅ Comments with timestamps and activity log
  - ✅ Subtasks
  - ✅ Board and List views with toggle
  - ✅ Search (by title, description, or card number)
  - ✅ Advanced filters (priority, tags) with collapsible panel
  - ✅ Archived tasks view
  - ✅ Quick Add modal (Cmd/Ctrl+K global shortcut)
  - ✅ Keyboard navigation (arrow keys, Enter, Delete, ? for help)
  - ✅ Undo/Redo with toast notifications - **UNIQUE ADVANTAGE**
  - ✅ Auto-save on blur
  - ✅ View mode persistence (localStorage)
  - ✅ Effort estimates (XS/S/M/L/XL)
  - ✅ Custom status support
  - ✅ Time estimates and tracking integration
  - ✅ Custom fields integration
  - ✅ Recurring tasks integration
  - ✅ Task dependencies (blocks/blocked-by relationships)
- **Critical Gaps (Preventing 100%+ Parity):**
  1. ✅ **Assignees/Members** - ✅ COMPLETE (Verified Nov 29, 2025)
     - ✅ Multiple assignees per task (like Trello/Monday.com)
     - ✅ Member management in Settings (add/edit/delete)
     - ✅ AssigneePicker component with multi-select
     - ✅ Avatar display on Kanban cards (max 3, +N indicator)
     - ✅ Assignee filter in Kanban view
     - **Files:** `src/types/index.ts` (Member interface), `src/components/AssigneePicker.tsx`, `src/components/MemberSettings.tsx`
     - **Documented:** `docs/product/feature_map.md` lines 201-207
  2. ✅ **File Attachments** - ✅ COMPLETE (Verified Nov 29, 2025)
     - ✅ Drag-drop file upload with validation (max 10MB)
     - ✅ Image preview with thumbnails
     - ✅ Download and delete actions
     - ✅ File type icons and size formatting
     - ✅ Base64 encoding for IndexedDB storage
     - **Files:** `src/components/FileUpload.tsx`, `src/components/AttachmentList.tsx`, `src/stores/useKanbanStore.ts` (addAttachment/deleteAttachment)
     - **Documented:** `docs/product/feature_map.md` line 1152
  3. ✅ **WIP Limits** - ✅ COMPLETE (Implemented Nov 29, 2025)
     - ✅ Set WIP limit per column via ColumnManager
     - ✅ Visual indicators: yellow (at limit), red (over limit), count badge (e.g., "5 / 8")
     - ✅ Soft limits (default): warnings on drag, allows move
     - ✅ Hard limits (optional): prevents dragging into full columns
     - ✅ Enforcement toggle in Settings ("Enforce WIP limits strictly")
     - **Files:** `src/types/index.ts` (KanbanColumn.wipLimit), `src/widgets/Kanban.tsx` (handleDragEnd validation), `src/pages/Settings.tsx` (enforcement toggle), `src/stores/useSettingsStore.ts` (enforceWipLimits state)
     - **Phase:** 3.3 (WIP Limits Enforcement)
  4. ✅ **Card Covers/Images** - ✅ COMPLETE (Implemented Nov 29, 2025)
     - ✅ Image attachments display as card covers (120px height, 16:9 aspect ratio)
     - ✅ First image attachment automatically used as cover
     - ✅ Fit mode (default): show full image with letterbox (object-contain)
     - ✅ Fill mode: crop to fill card width (object-cover)
     - ✅ Cover mode selector in CardDetailPanel Attachments tab
     - ✅ Lazy loading with loading="lazy" for performance
     - ✅ No cover when no image attachments (only non-image files)
     - **Files:** `src/types/index.ts` (Task.coverMode), `src/widgets/Kanban/KanbanCard.tsx` (cover display), `src/widgets/Kanban/CardDetailPanel.tsx` (mode selector)
     - **Phase:** 3.4 (Card Covers)
     - **Documented:** `docs/product/feature_map.md` lines 217-225
  5. ⚠️ **Calendar View Integration** - Calendar exists but on separate page
     - Trello: Premium feature, integrated view
     - Asana: ✅ One-click view switch
     - Monday.com: ✅ Instant view toggle
     - **Impact:** Less convenient workflow, requires navigation
     - **Priority:** P2 (UX improvement, not blocker)
- **Our Unique Advantages:**
  - ✅ Card numbers (KAN-#) - Unique to us, professional tracking
  - ✅ Undo/Redo - Unique to us, prevents mistakes

input.content:
# P0 Forms Advanced Features

**Priority:** P0 (Critical - blocking professional usage)
**Estimated Effort:** 8 hours
**Impact:** Forms currently have baseline functionality but missing critical features that prevent professional usage (conditional logic, file uploads, progress indicators, analytics, spam protection)
**Date Created:** 2025-11-29
**Status:** IN PROGRESS

---

## Overview

Forms currently support 11 field types with validation, but are missing critical professional features that ALL competitors (Google Forms, Typeform, Tally) provide. These gaps prevent forms from being usable for complex workflows (surveys, job applications, assessments, etc.).

**Critical P0 Gaps (8 identified, implementing 5 now):**
1. ❌ **Conditional Logic** - Can't show/hide fields based on answers (ALL competitors have this)
2. ❌ **File Upload** - Can't collect documents/images (ALL competitors have this)
3. ❌ **Progress Bar** - Users can't see completion status (ALL competitors have this)
4. ❌ **Response Analytics** - No insights into form performance (ALL competitors have this)
5. ❌ **Spam Protection** - No honeypot/captcha (Typeform/Tally have this)
6. ⏳ **Answer Piping** - Reference previous answers in questions (Typeform/Tally - defer to P2)
7. ⏳ **Hidden Fields** - UTM parameter tracking (Typeform/Tally - defer to P2)
8. ⏳ **Quiz Mode** - Auto-scoring/feedback (Google Forms/Typeform/Tally - defer to P2)

**This task focuses on P0 gaps 1-5** which are baseline functionality required for ANY professional form builder.

---

## Requirements

### Phase 1: Conditional Logic (4 hrs)
**Goal:** Show/hide fields based on previous answers

**Scope:**
- Add `conditionalLogic` property to `FormField` interface
- Support basic conditions:
  - Field X equals value Y → show Field Z
  - Field X NOT equals value Y → hide Field Z
  - Field X is answered → show Field Z
  - Multiple AND/OR conditions
- UI in FormBuilder to configure conditional rules
- Runtime evaluation in FormFiller to show/hide fields dynamically
- Validation: required fields hidden by conditions should not block submission

**Technical Approach:**
```typescript
// Types
interface ConditionalRule {
  id: string;
  fieldId: string; // Which field triggers this rule
  operator: 'equals' | 'not_equals' | 'contains' | 'is_answered' | 'is_not_answered';
  value?: any; // Value to compare against
  action: 'show' | 'hide';
}

interface FormField {
  // ... existing properties
  conditionalRules?: ConditionalRule[]; // Rules that determine if this field is shown
}
```

**Files to Modify:**
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add ConditionalRule interface, extend FormField
- `src/pages/FormBuilder.tsx` - Add conditional logic UI in field editor
- `src/pages/FormFiller.tsx` - Implement runtime evaluation logic
- `src/components/ConditionalRuleEditor.tsx` - NEW: UI for configuring rules

**Validation Logic:**
- Evaluate rules top-to-bottom (first match wins)
- Support AND conditions (all must be true)
- Hidden required fields don't block submission
- Circular dependency detection (A depends on B depends on A)

---

### Phase 2: File Upload (2 hrs)
**Goal:** Add file upload field type with storage

**Scope:**
- New field type: `'file'`
- File validation: max size (10MB), allowed types (images, PDFs, docs)
- Storage: Base64 encode files into FormResponse.answers
- File preview in FormResponses view
- Download capability

**Technical Approach:**
```typescript
// Types
export type FieldType =
  | 'text'
  | 'number'
  | 'date'
  | 'time'
  | 'select'
  | 'multiselect'
  | 'checkbox'
  | 'radio'
  | 'textarea'
  | 'rating'
  | 'scale'
  | 'file'; // NEW

interface FormField {
  // ... existing properties
  fileConfig?: {
    maxSizeMB: number; // Default: 10MB
    allowedTypes: string[]; // MIME types: ['image/*', 'application/pdf']
    multiple: boolean; // Allow multiple file uploads
  };
}

interface FileUploadAnswer {
  fileName: string;
  fileType: string;
  fileSize: number;
  base64Data: string; // Base64 encoded file content
  uploadedAt: Date;
}
```

**Files to Modify:**
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add 'file' to FieldType, add fileConfig interface
- `src/pages/FormBuilder.tsx` - Add file field configuration UI
- `src/pages/FormFiller.tsx` - Add file upload input with validation
- `src/pages/FormResponses.tsx` - Add file preview/download in response view
- `src/components/FileUploadField.tsx` - NEW: Reusable file upload component

**Storage Considerations:**
- Base64 encoding increases file size by ~33%
- 10MB file → ~13MB stored → acceptable for IndexedDB
- Alternative: Store in separate IndexedDB object store (optimization for P2)

---

### Phase 3: Progress Bar (1 hr)
**Goal:** Show completion percentage and progress indicator

**Scope:**
- Calculate progress: (answered fields / total visible fields) × 100
- Show progress bar at top of form
- Show percentage text
- Account for conditional logic (hidden fields don't count)

**Technical Approach:**
```typescript
// FormFiller.tsx
function calculateProgress(fields: FormField[], answers: Record<string, any>, visibleFieldIds: Set<string>): number {
  const visibleFields = fields.filter(f => visibleFieldIds.has(f.id));
  const answeredFields = visibleFields.filter(f => {
    const answer = answers[f.id];
    return answer !== undefined && answer !== '' && answer !== null;
  });

  return visibleFields.length > 0
    ? Math.round((answeredFields.length / visibleFields.length) * 100)
    : 0;
}
```

**UI Design:**
- Fixed position at top of form (sticky)
- Gradient progress bar (magenta accent)
- Percentage text (e.g., "60% complete")
- Smooth animation on progress changes

**Files to Modify:**
- `src/pages/FormFiller.tsx` - Add progress calculation and bar UI
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add `showProgressBar` to FormSettings

---

### Phase 4: Analytics (1 hr)
**Goal:** Basic response metrics and insights

**Scope:**
- Completion rate: (submitted / started) × 100
- Average completion time
- Field-level metrics:
  - Most common answers (for select/radio)
  - Average rating/scale values
  - Response distribution charts
- Time-based analytics:
  - Responses per day/week/month
  - Peak submission times

**Technical Approach:**
```typescript
// Analytics data structure
interface FormAnalytics {
  formId: string;
  totalResponses: number;
  completionRate: number; // % of users who started and finished
  avgCompletionTimeSeconds: number;
  responsesByDay: Record<string, number>; // YYYY-MM-DD -> count
  fieldAnalytics: Record<string, FieldAnalytics>;
}

interface FieldAnalytics {
  fieldId: string;
  fieldType: FieldType;
  responseCount: number; // How many people answered this field
  // Type-specific metrics
  mostCommonAnswers?: Array<{ value: string; count: number }>; // For select/radio
  averageValue?: number; // For rating/scale/number
  distribution?: Record<string, number>; // Value -> count
}
```

**UI Components:**
- Analytics tab in FormResponses page
- Metric cards (total responses, completion rate, avg time)
- Charts using Recharts:
  - Bar chart: Responses over time
  - Pie chart: Field answer distribution
  - Line chart: Completion rate trend

**Files to Modify:**
- `src/pages/FormResponses.tsx` - Add Analytics tab with charts
- `src/services/formAnalytics.ts` - NEW: Analytics calculation service
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add FormAnalytics interface

---

### Phase 5: Spam Protection (30 min)
**Goal:** Basic spam prevention (honeypot technique)

**Scope:**
- Honeypot field (hidden from humans, visible to bots)
- Time-based validation (submissions <2 seconds = suspicious)
- Flag suspicious responses in FormResponses view

**Technical Approach:**
```typescript
// Honeypot implementation
interface FormResponse {
  // ... existing properties
  isSpam?: boolean; // Flag for suspicious responses
  submissionTimeSeconds?: number; // Time from load to submit
}

// FormFiller.tsx
function FormFiller() {
  const [formLoadTime] = useState(Date.now());
  const [honeypot, setHoneypot] = useState('');

  const handleSubmit = () => {
    const submissionTime = (Date.now() - formLoadTime) / 1000;
    const isSpam = honeypot !== '' || submissionTime < 2;

    submitResponse(formId, {
      ...answers,
      isSpam,
      submissionTimeSeconds: submissionTime,
    });
  };
}
```

**Honeypot Field:**
- Hidden with `position: absolute; left: -9999px`
- Named "email_confirm" or similar (common bot target)
- Not visible to users (CSS, not `display: none` which bots detect)

**Files to Modify:**
- `src/pages/FormFiller.tsx` - Add honeypot field and timing
- `src/pages/FormResponses.tsx` - Show spam flag in response list
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add isSpam/submissionTimeSeconds to FormResponse
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add `enableSpamProtection` to FormSettings

---

## Implementation Plan

### Order of Execution
1. **Phase 1: Conditional Logic** (highest complexity, most impactful)
2. **Phase 2: File Upload** (moderate complexity, baseline feature)
3. **Phase 3: Progress Bar** (low complexity, quick UX win)
4. **Phase 4: Analytics** (moderate complexity, valuable insights)
5. **Phase 5: Spam Protection** (low complexity, security hardening)

### Verification Steps
After each phase:
1. Test in FormBuilder (can configure feature)
2. Test in FormFiller (feature works at runtime)
3. Test in FormResponses (data displays correctly)
4. Run `npm run test && npm run type-check`

---

## Files to Create

1. `src/components/ConditionalRuleEditor.tsx` - UI for configuring conditional rules
2. `src/components/FileUploadField.tsx` - Reusable file upload component
3. `src/services/formAnalytics.ts` - Analytics calculation service

---

## Files to Modify

### Type Definitions
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add ConditionalRule, extend FieldType, add analytics interfaces

### Pages
- `src/pages/FormBuilder.tsx` - Add UIs for conditional logic, file config
- `src/pages/FormFiller.tsx` - Add conditional evaluation, file upload, progress bar, honeypot
- `src/pages/FormResponses.tsx` - Add Analytics tab, file preview, spam indicators

---

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
- Conditional logic evaluation (edge cases: circular deps, AND/OR logic)
- File validation (size limits, MIME types)
- Progress calculation (with/without conditional fields)
- Analytics calculations (aggregations, averages, distributions)
- Spam detection (honeypot, timing)

### Manual Testing
1. Create form with conditional logic (if Q1=Yes, show Q2)
2. Upload files (images, PDFs) and verify storage/retrieval
3. Fill form and verify progress bar updates correctly
4. Submit multiple responses and check analytics accuracy
5. Test spam protection (fill honeypot, submit in <2 seconds)

---

## Key Decisions

### Conditional Logic Approach
**Decision:** Use declarative rules instead of JavaScript expressions
- **Rationale:** More secure (no eval), easier to debug, UI-configurable
- **Tradeoff:** Less flexible than scripting, but covers 90% of use cases

### File Storage Strategy
**Decision:** Base64 encode into FormResponse.answers (same as Kanban attachments)
- **Rationale:** Simple, consistent with existing patterns, works with IndexedDB
- **Tradeoff:** 33% size overhead, but acceptable for 10MB limit
- **Future:** Can optimize to separate object store if needed

### Analytics Scope
**Decision:** Focus on completion rates and field-level metrics (NOT funnel analysis)
- **Rationale:** Funnel requires session tracking beyond current scope
- **Tradeoff:** Less advanced than Typeform, but covers core use cases

### Spam Protection Level
**Decision:** Basic honeypot + timing (NOT reCAPTCHA)
- **Rationale:** No external dependencies, privacy-first, catches 80% of bots
- **Tradeoff:** Not as robust as reCAPTCHA, but acceptable for local-first app

---

## P2 Deferred Features

These are valuable but not blocking:
- **Answer Piping** - Reference previous answers in questions (P2 - 2hrs)
- **Hidden Fields** - UTM parameter tracking (P2 - 1hr)
- **Quiz Mode** - Auto-scoring and feedback (P2 - 3hrs)
- **Advanced Analytics** - Funnel analysis, drop-off points (P2 - 4hrs)
- **reCAPTCHA Integration** - Google reCAPTCHA (P2 - 2hrs, requires API key)

---

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Conditional logic works in FormBuilder and FormFiller
- [ ] File upload field type supports images/PDFs with 10MB limit

toolUseResult.content:
# P0 Forms Advanced Features

**Priority:** P0 (Critical - blocking professional usage)
**Estimated Effort:** 8 hours
**Impact:** Forms currently have baseline functionality but missing critical features that prevent professional usage (conditional logic, file uploads, progress indicators, analytics, spam protection)
**Date Created:** 2025-11-29
**Status:** IN PROGRESS

---

## Overview

Forms currently support 11 field types with validation, but are missing critical professional features that ALL competitors (Google Forms, Typeform, Tally) provide. These gaps prevent forms from being usable for complex workflows (surveys, job applications, assessments, etc.).

**Critical P0 Gaps (8 identified, implementing 5 now):**
1. ❌ **Conditional Logic** - Can't show/hide fields based on answers (ALL competitors have this)
2. ❌ **File Upload** - Can't collect documents/images (ALL competitors have this)
3. ❌ **Progress Bar** - Users can't see completion status (ALL competitors have this)
4. ❌ **Response Analytics** - No insights into form performance (ALL competitors have this)
5. ❌ **Spam Protection** - No honeypot/captcha (Typeform/Tally have this)
6. ⏳ **Answer Piping** - Reference previous answers in questions (Typeform/Tally - defer to P2)
7. ⏳ **Hidden Fields** - UTM parameter tracking (Typeform/Tally - defer to P2)
8. ⏳ **Quiz Mode** - Auto-scoring/feedback (Google Forms/Typeform/Tally - defer to P2)

**This task focuses on P0 gaps 1-5** which are baseline functionality required for ANY professional form builder.

---

## Requirements

### Phase 1: Conditional Logic (4 hrs)
**Goal:** Show/hide fields based on previous answers

**Scope:**
- Add `conditionalLogic` property to `FormField` interface
- Support basic conditions:
  - Field X equals value Y → show Field Z
  - Field X NOT equals value Y → hide Field Z
  - Field X is answered → show Field Z
  - Multiple AND/OR conditions
- UI in FormBuilder to configure conditional rules
- Runtime evaluation in FormFiller to show/hide fields dynamically
- Validation: required fields hidden by conditions should not block submission

**Technical Approach:**
```typescript
// Types
interface ConditionalRule {
  id: string;
  fieldId: string; // Which field triggers this rule
  operator: 'equals' | 'not_equals' | 'contains' | 'is_answered' | 'is_not_answered';
  value?: any; // Value to compare against
  action: 'show' | 'hide';
}

interface FormField {
  // ... existing properties
  conditionalRules?: ConditionalRule[]; // Rules that determine if this field is shown
}
```

**Files to Modify:**
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add ConditionalRule interface, extend FormField
- `src/pages/FormBuilder.tsx` - Add conditional logic UI in field editor
- `src/pages/FormFiller.tsx` - Implement runtime evaluation logic
- `src/components/ConditionalRuleEditor.tsx` - NEW: UI for configuring rules

**Validation Logic:**
- Evaluate rules top-to-bottom (first match wins)
- Support AND conditions (all must be true)
- Hidden required fields don't block submission
- Circular dependency detection (A depends on B depends on A)

---

### Phase 2: File Upload (2 hrs)
**Goal:** Add file upload field type with storage

**Scope:**
- New field type: `'file'`
- File validation: max size (10MB), allowed types (images, PDFs, docs)
- Storage: Base64 encode files into FormResponse.answers
- File preview in FormResponses view
- Download capability

**Technical Approach:**
```typescript
// Types
export type FieldType =
  | 'text'
  | 'number'
  | 'date'
  | 'time'
  | 'select'
  | 'multiselect'
  | 'checkbox'
  | 'radio'
  | 'textarea'
  | 'rating'
  | 'scale'
  | 'file'; // NEW

interface FormField {
  // ... existing properties
  fileConfig?: {
    maxSizeMB: number; // Default: 10MB
    allowedTypes: string[]; // MIME types: ['image/*', 'application/pdf']
    multiple: boolean; // Allow multiple file uploads
  };
}

interface FileUploadAnswer {
  fileName: string;
  fileType: string;
  fileSize: number;
  base64Data: string; // Base64 encoded file content
  uploadedAt: Date;
}
```

**Files to Modify:**
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add 'file' to FieldType, add fileConfig interface
- `src/pages/FormBuilder.tsx` - Add file field configuration UI
- `src/pages/FormFiller.tsx` - Add file upload input with validation
- `src/pages/FormResponses.tsx` - Add file preview/download in response view
- `src/components/FileUploadField.tsx` - NEW: Reusable file upload component

**Storage Considerations:**
- Base64 encoding increases file size by ~33%
- 10MB file → ~13MB stored → acceptable for IndexedDB
- Alternative: Store in separate IndexedDB object store (optimization for P2)

---

### Phase 3: Progress Bar (1 hr)
**Goal:** Show completion percentage and progress indicator

**Scope:**
- Calculate progress: (answered fields / total visible fields) × 100
- Show progress bar at top of form
- Show percentage text
- Account for conditional logic (hidden fields don't count)

**Technical Approach:**
```typescript
// FormFiller.tsx
function calculateProgress(fields: FormField[], answers: Record<string, any>, visibleFieldIds: Set<string>): number {
  const visibleFields = fields.filter(f => visibleFieldIds.has(f.id));
  const answeredFields = visibleFields.filter(f => {
    const answer = answers[f.id];
    return answer !== undefined && answer !== '' && answer !== null;
  });

  return visibleFields.length > 0
    ? Math.round((answeredFields.length / visibleFields.length) * 100)
    : 0;
}
```

**UI Design:**
- Fixed position at top of form (sticky)
- Gradient progress bar (magenta accent)
- Percentage text (e.g., "60% complete")
- Smooth animation on progress changes

**Files to Modify:**
- `src/pages/FormFiller.tsx` - Add progress calculation and bar UI
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add `showProgressBar` to FormSettings

---

### Phase 4: Analytics (1 hr)
**Goal:** Basic response metrics and insights

**Scope:**
- Completion rate: (submitted / started) × 100
- Average completion time
- Field-level metrics:
  - Most common answers (for select/radio)
  - Average rating/scale values
  - Response distribution charts
- Time-based analytics:
  - Responses per day/week/month
  - Peak submission times

**Technical Approach:**
```typescript
// Analytics data structure
interface FormAnalytics {
  formId: string;
  totalResponses: number;
  completionRate: number; // % of users who started and finished
  avgCompletionTimeSeconds: number;
  responsesByDay: Record<string, number>; // YYYY-MM-DD -> count
  fieldAnalytics: Record<string, FieldAnalytics>;
}

interface FieldAnalytics {
  fieldId: string;
  fieldType: FieldType;
  responseCount: number; // How many people answered this field
  // Type-specific metrics
  mostCommonAnswers?: Array<{ value: string; count: number }>; // For select/radio
  averageValue?: number; // For rating/scale/number
  distribution?: Record<string, number>; // Value -> count
}
```

**UI Components:**
- Analytics tab in FormResponses page
- Metric cards (total responses, completion rate, avg time)
- Charts using Recharts:
  - Bar chart: Responses over time
  - Pie chart: Field answer distribution
  - Line chart: Completion rate trend

**Files to Modify:**
- `src/pages/FormResponses.tsx` - Add Analytics tab with charts
- `src/services/formAnalytics.ts` - NEW: Analytics calculation service
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add FormAnalytics interface

---

### Phase 5: Spam Protection (30 min)
**Goal:** Basic spam prevention (honeypot technique)

**Scope:**
- Honeypot field (hidden from humans, visible to bots)
- Time-based validation (submissions <2 seconds = suspicious)
- Flag suspicious responses in FormResponses view

**Technical Approach:**
```typescript
// Honeypot implementation
interface FormResponse {
  // ... existing properties
  isSpam?: boolean; // Flag for suspicious responses
  submissionTimeSeconds?: number; // Time from load to submit
}

// FormFiller.tsx
function FormFiller() {
  const [formLoadTime] = useState(Date.now());
  const [honeypot, setHoneypot] = useState('');

  const handleSubmit = () => {
    const submissionTime = (Date.now() - formLoadTime) / 1000;
    const isSpam = honeypot !== '' || submissionTime < 2;

    submitResponse(formId, {
      ...answers,
      isSpam,
      submissionTimeSeconds: submissionTime,
    });
  };
}
```

**Honeypot Field:**
- Hidden with `position: absolute; left: -9999px`
- Named "email_confirm" or similar (common bot target)
- Not visible to users (CSS, not `display: none` which bots detect)

**Files to Modify:**
- `src/pages/FormFiller.tsx` - Add honeypot field and timing
- `src/pages/FormResponses.tsx` - Show spam flag in response list
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add isSpam/submissionTimeSeconds to FormResponse
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add `enableSpamProtection` to FormSettings

---

## Implementation Plan

### Order of Execution
1. **Phase 1: Conditional Logic** (highest complexity, most impactful)
2. **Phase 2: File Upload** (moderate complexity, baseline feature)
3. **Phase 3: Progress Bar** (low complexity, quick UX win)
4. **Phase 4: Analytics** (moderate complexity, valuable insights)
5. **Phase 5: Spam Protection** (low complexity, security hardening)

### Verification Steps
After each phase:
1. Test in FormBuilder (can configure feature)
2. Test in FormFiller (feature works at runtime)
3. Test in FormResponses (data displays correctly)
4. Run `npm run test && npm run type-check`

---

## Files to Create

1. `src/components/ConditionalRuleEditor.tsx` - UI for configuring conditional rules
2. `src/components/FileUploadField.tsx` - Reusable file upload component
3. `src/services/formAnalytics.ts` - Analytics calculation service

---

## Files to Modify

### Type Definitions
- `src/types/forms.ts` - Add ConditionalRule, extend FieldType, add analytics interfaces

### Pages
- `src/pages/FormBuilder.tsx` - Add UIs for conditional logic, file config
- `src/pages/FormFiller.tsx` - Add conditional evaluation, file upload, progress bar, honeypot
- `src/pages/FormResponses.tsx` - Add Analytics tab, file preview, spam indicators

---

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
- Conditional logic evaluation (edge cases: circular deps, AND/OR logic)
- File validation (size limits, MIME types)
- Progress calculation (with/without conditional fields)
- Analytics calculations (aggregations, averages, distributions)
- Spam detection (honeypot, timing)

### Manual Testing
1. Create form with conditional logic (if Q1=Yes, show Q2)
2. Upload files (images, PDFs) and verify storage/retrieval
3. Fill form and verify progress bar updates correctly
4. Submit multiple responses and check analytics accuracy
5. Test spam protection (fill honeypot, submit in <2 seconds)

---

## Key Decisions

### Conditional Logic Approach
**Decision:** Use declarative rules instead of JavaScript expressions
- **Rationale:** More secure (no eval), easier to debug, UI-configurable
- **Tradeoff:** Less flexible than scripting, but covers 90% of use cases

### File Storage Strategy
**Decision:** Base64 encode into FormResponse.answers (same as Kanban attachments)
- **Rationale:** Simple, consistent with existing patterns, works with IndexedDB
- **Tradeoff:** 33% size overhead, but acceptable for 10MB limit
- **Future:** Can optimize to separate object store if needed

### Analytics Scope
**Decision:** Focus on completion rates and field-level metrics (NOT funnel analysis)
- **Rationale:** Funnel requires session tracking beyond current scope
- **Tradeoff:** Less advanced than Typeform, but covers core use cases

### Spam Protection Level
**Decision:** Basic honeypot + timing (NOT reCAPTCHA)
- **Rationale:** No external dependencies, privacy-first, catches 80% of bots
- **Tradeoff:** Not as robust as reCAPTCHA, but acceptable for local-first app

---

## P2 Deferred Features

These are valuable but not blocking:
- **Answer Piping** - Reference previous answers in questions (P2 - 2hrs)
- **Hidden Fields** - UTM parameter tracking (P2 - 1hr)
- **Quiz Mode** - Auto-scoring and feedback (P2 - 3hrs)
- **Advanced Analytics** - Funnel analysis, drop-off points (P2 - 4hrs)
- **reCAPTCHA Integration** - Google reCAPTCHA (P2 - 2hrs, requires API key)

---

## Definition of Done

- [ ] Conditional logic works in FormBuilder and FormFiller
- [ ] File upload field type supports images/PDFs with 10MB limit
- [ ] Progress bar shows accurate completion percentage
- [ ] Analytics tab displays response metrics and charts
- [ ] Spam protection flags suspicious responses
- [ ] All tests pass (`npm run test && npm run type-check`)
- [ ] Task file updated with implementation review
- [ ] Committed with detailed message
- [ ] Archived to `.archive/completed-tasks/`
- [ ] `master-implementation-status.md` updated to mark P0 gap as CLOSED

---

## Sources

- [Google Forms Features](https://www.google.com/forms/about/)
- [Typeform Pricing & Features](https://www.typeform.com/pricing/)
- [Tally Features](https://tally.so/features) - FREE conditional logic, file uploads, hidden fields
- [Form UX Best Practices](https://www.nngroup.com/articles/web-form-design/)
- [Honeypot Spam Prevention](https://www.sitepoint.com/easy-spam-prevention-using-hidden-form-fields/)
